summary: Sidebar navigation is unified into one reorderable Menu: maintenance sections are single entries with internal tab bars, Duplicates Finder splits Find and Review into tabs, and Activity Log and Settings join the main menu.
introduced: Unified Menu sidebar — Disk Analysis, Duplicates Finder, Clean My Mac, Developer Projects, System Cleanup, System Optimization, Activity Log, and Settings share one drag-to-reorder list

Maintenance tab menus — Clean My Mac, Developer Projects, and System Cleanup each open as a menu item with a description and icon tab bar for their tools (caches, node_modules, APFS snapshots, and more)
Duplicates Finder tabs — Find Duplicates and Review Duplicates are separate tabs inside Duplicates Finder instead of a sidebar shortcut
changed: Review Duplicates moved into Duplicates Finder — scan from Find Duplicates; results and cleanup actions live under Review Duplicates
Activity Log and Settings moved from the Actions section into the main Menu alongside other destinations
Maintenance sidebar — individual cleanup tools are no longer listed separately; pick a section from Menu, then switch tools via tabs
